text: In twenty years, the USSR will be producing almost twice as much industrial output as is now produced in the entire non socialist world.
author: Nikita Sergeyevich Khrushchev
source: Speech at the 22nd Congress of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union

didItHoldUp.analysis: By 1981, the Soviet economy was stagnating, not surging. Instead of doubling the non-socialist world's output, the USSR was falling further behind — its GDP was roughly a quarter of America's alone. Within 30 years of this speech, the Soviet Union would cease to exist entirely. Khrushchev's prediction is a masterclass in how ideological conviction distorts economic forecasting. He genuinely believed that central planning would outperform market economies, and he had the Sputnik-era momentum to make it sound plausible. The numbers told a very different story.
